label: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 13

extraction.one_line_summary: VINGEGAARD attacked 8km from summit of Tourmalet with AYUSO, dropped everyone including ROGLIČ, KUSS policed then took second, Jumbo-Visma occupies podium.
tactical_patterns: VINGEGAARD attacked with AYUSO at 8km from summit, forcing KUSS to police rather than chase his own leader.
KUSS repeatedly changed pace to exhaust rivals before chasing VINGEGAARD late, securing team podium lockout over stage win.
EVENEPOEL dropped on first climb Aubisque despite not being ill, suggesting altitude/accumulation broke him unexpectedly early.
LANDA isolated late on Tourmalet with no UAE support while Jumbo had KUSS and ROGLIČ numerical advantage.

rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: Won stage and took mountains jersey despite helping ROGLIČ at Tour, shows form independence from team hierarchy
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: Played policeman role then bridged 50-second gap to take second, revealing hidden acceleration reserves
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: Marked moves but didn't follow VINGEGAARD attack, accepting team tactics over personal stage ambition
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: negative: Dropped on Aubisque over 100km from finish, losing 27 minutes, GC challenge collapsed without illness excuse
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: positive: Only rider able to follow VINGEGAARD's 8km attack initially, retained white jersey despite youth
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: negative: Isolated and distanced on Tourmalet when attacks came, lacks team support for podium challenge
rider_signals.UIJTDEBROEKS Cian: positive: Present in quality breakaway and late on Tourmalet, showing Grand Tour legs as neo-pro
rider_signals.MARTINEZ Lenny: neutral: In breakaway but faded on Tourmalet, stage win earlier may have taken toll
rider_signals.MAS Enric: negative: Started second overall, tumbled significantly, age/accumulation suggested as weakness factor
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: neutral: Finished with MAS group, lost significant time from early GC position
